Short TitleFor short title of title VI of , which enacted this part as the “Health Care Services in the Home Act of 1987”, see , set out as a Short Title of 1987 Amendments note under .Pub. L. 100–175section 601 of Pub. L. 100–175section 201 of this title

children.text: to identify low-income individuals who can avoid institutionalization or prolonged hospitalization if skilled medical services, skilled nursing care services, homemaker or home health aide services, or personal care services are provided in the homes of the individuals;
to pay the costs of the provision of such services in the homes of such individuals; and
to coordinate the provision by public and private entities of such services, and other long-term care services, in the homes of such individuals.

children.heading: Requirement with respect to age of recipients of services
children.label: b Requirement with respect to age of recipients of services

children.text: not less than 25 percent of the grant is expended to provide services under such subsection to individuals who are not less than 65 years of age; and

of the portion of the grant reserved by the State for purposes of complying with paragraph (1), not less than 10 percent is expended to provide such services to individuals who are not less than 85 years of age.

children.heading: Relationship to items and services under other programs
children.label: c Relationship to items and services under other programs

children.text: under any State compensation program, under an insurance policy, or under any Federal or State health benefits program; or
by an entity that provides health services on a prepaid basis.
